content: The 2024 SOTA hybrid episodic-semantic agent memory design of Synapse can be reframed as a native scalable shared memory layer for multi-agent active learning, where agents collectively prune redundant memory and prioritize high-information unlabeled data to query directly via shared episodic traces. This architecture eliminates the common bottleneck of redundant per-agent memory storage and uncoordinated querying in large multi-agent active learning pools.
source_topics: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
trigger_topic: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
